summary: Accepted M2-MAP-SOURCE-POLICY-001 after historical_researcher wrote a concise M2 prototype map fixture source policy and qa_reviewer independently accepted it. This acceptance covers source-policy documentation only and does not approve production historical map content, named settlements, polity claims, cultural symbols, or content-source data.

tests_run.result: M2-MAP-SOURCE-POLICY-001 was REVIEW before lead status update; other M2 tasks remained DRAFT except the CLOSED graph task.
No output while M2-MAP-SOURCE-POLICY-001 remained in REVIEW.
Policy contains HISTORICAL, INFERRED, COMPOSITE, FICTIONAL, source_category, confidence, coordinate_geometry_derivation_note, known_limitations, Bad-Map Fixtures, and validation data only.

tests_run.command: node -e thread registry duplicate ID and route record check
tests_run.result: 37 registry entries with unique thread IDs; M2-MAP-SOURCE-POLICY route records include required to_thread_id and send_input submission ID.
format:check, lint, typecheck, and architecture:check passed.

decisions: M2 prototype map fixture data defaults to FICTIONAL and synthetic until a separate historical research/content task authorizes formal historical map content.
The policy blocks real names, real symbols, polity control, cultural identity, and historical settlement claims from entering M2 fixtures without source and claim records.
Bad-map fixtures are validation data only, not production, tutorial, screenshot, marketing, or narrative placeholder content.
risks: Later M2 content/compiler tasks must still enforce this policy in schemas, fixtures, and validation tests.
This task intentionally does not decide exact historical geography or cultural claims.
requested_action: Commit, push, open PR, and integrate M2-MAP-SOURCE-POLICY-001 if final gates pass.
